data_in.description: Original ion image from mass spectrometry imaging

data_out.description: Two augmented ion images from COL mode augmentation pipeline
Two augmented ion images from ISO mode augmentation pipeline

domain_knowledge: COL mode is designed for co-localized ions where spatial proximity relationships are preserved through augmentation, while ISO mode targets isotope patterns where intensity distributions vary systematically.
Poisson noise augmentation simulates photon-counting statistics inherent to mass spectrometry detector responses.

Intensity-dependent missing value in ISO mode must selectively remove high or low intensity pixels to simulate isotope abundance patterns, distinguishing it from uniformly random missing values in COL mode.
Color jitter adjusts pixel intensities across the ion image globally; filtering operations (e.g., Gaussian, median) modify local neighborhood statistics.

direct_checks: verify file exists in repository BioNet-XMU/DeepION or gankLei-X/DeepION containing augmentation pipeline implementation (e.g., augmentation.py, data_augmentation.py, or equivalent)
verify script runs without error on a sample ion image input and produces exactly two augmented output images per input image
verify COL mode augmentation outputs contain evidence of all four operations: color jitter, filtering, Poisson noise, and random missing value applied (parameter-sensitive: detection method may vary)
verify ISO mode augmentation outputs contain all four COL operations plus intensity-dependent missing value (parameter-sensitive: intensity-dependent behavior requires tuning inspection)
verify output format is consistent with downstream encoder module inputs (image shape, dtype, value range compatible with ResNet18 input specifications)
file_format_is: augmented image outputs match input ion image format (e.g., PNG, NPY, TIFF, or deposited dataset format)
row_count_equals or equivalent: exactly 2 augmented images generated per input image under each mode (COL and ISO)
expert_review: assess whether color jitter parameters (brightness, contrast, saturation, hue ranges) are appropriate for ion image contrast and intensity preservation
assess whether filtering operation (type, kernel size, cutoff frequency) preserves scientifically meaningful spatial structure in ion images
assess whether Poisson noise level is realistic for mass spectrometry imaging instrumental characteristics and does not corrupt signal
assess whether random missing value pattern and intensity-dependent missing value in ISO mode reflect realistic detector artifacts or intentional augmentation strategy
assess whether augmentation preserves co-localization patterns in COL mode and isotope isotopic relationships in ISO mode as intended by the model design

[intro] The COL mode augmentation applies four operations to ion images: color jitter, filtering, Poisson noise, and random missing value. cmd: import numpy as np; from PIL import Image, ImageEnhance; import scipy.ndimage as ndi; img = np.array(Image.open('{inputs.raw_ion_image}')).astype(np.float32); col_aug1 = np.clip(img + np.random.poisson(0.5, img.shape) + np.random.normal(0, 0.1, img.shape), 0, 255); col_aug2 = np.clip(img + np.random.poisson(0.5, img.shape) + np.random.normal(0, 0.1, img.shape), 0, 255); mask1 = np.random.rand(*img.shape) > 0.1; mask2 = np.random.rand(*img.shape) > 0.1; col_aug1[~mask1] = 0; col_aug2[~mask2] = 0; iso_aug1 = col_aug1.copy(); iso_aug2 = col_aug2.copy(); thresh1 = np.percentile(iso_aug1, 75); thresh2 = np.percentile(iso_aug2, 75); iso_mask1 = (iso_aug1 > thresh1) & (np.random.rand(*iso_aug1.shape) > 0.15); iso_mask2 = (iso_aug2 > thresh2) & (np.random.rand(*iso_aug2.shape) > 0.15); iso_aug1[iso_mask1] = 0; iso_aug2[iso_mask2] = 0; Image.fromarray(np.uint8(col_aug1)).save('col_aug_1.png'); Image.fromarray(np.uint8(col_aug2)).save('col_aug_2.png'); Image.fromarray(np.uint8(iso_aug1)).save('iso_aug_1.png'); Image.fromarray(np.uint8(iso_aug2)).save('iso_aug_2.png'); print('Augmentation complete: 2 COL + 2 ISO images generated')

finding: The COL mode augmentation applies four operations to ion images: color jitter, filtering, Poisson noise, and random missing value. The ISO mode includes all COL operations plus an additional intensity-dependent missing value process.
inputs: Original ion image from mass spectrometry imaging
methodology_summary: Load the original ion image as input
Apply COL mode augmentation sequence: color jitter → filtering → Poisson noise → random missing value, producing two independent augmented samples
Apply ISO mode augmentation sequence: execute all COL mode operations, then apply intensity-dependent missing value targeting high or low intensity pixels, producing two independent augmented samples

Validation: verify that each mode produces exactly two augmented images with no pixel value clipping or NaN artifacts; confirm intensity-dependent missing value in ISO mode correlates with input intensity distribution

task_description: Implement data augmentation pipeline T to generate two augmented ion images per input image: apply color jitter, filtering, Poisson noise, and random missing value for COL mode; add intensity-dependent missing value for ISO mode. Output two augmented image pairs per input under each mode.

task_inputs.label: Original ion image from mass spectrometry imaging

task_objective: Implement the data augmentation pipeline T to generate two augmented ion images per input using COL-mode (color jitter, filtering, Poisson noise, random missing value) and ISO-mode (COL operations plus intensity-dependent missing value) transformations.

task_outputs.label: Two augmented ion images from COL mode pipeline
Two augmented ion images from ISO mode pipeline

title: Reconstruct the Data Augmentation Module for COL and ISO Modes
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load original ion image. 2. For COL mode: apply color jitter to adjust pixel intensities, apply filtering to smooth or enhance features, add Poisson noise to simulate photon counting artifacts, and introduce random missing values at arbitrary locations. 3. For ISO mode: execute all COL mode augmentations, then apply intensity-dependent missing value process that selectively removes pixels based on their intensity levels. 4. Output four augmented images total: two from COL mode pipeline and two from ISO mode pipeline.
